seo.title: Fintech Content Generation AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for content generation AI agents in fintech. Covers SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR.

content.intro: Deploying AI content generation agents in fintech carries compounded risk: a single non-compliant output can trigger SEC enforcement, violate GDPR data handling rules, or constitute unlicensed financial advice at scale. This checklist operationalizes the intersection of LLM content pipelines and critical financial industry regulations—SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, BSA/AML, and SEC rules—so every generated asset is audit-ready before it reaches customers. Use it as a pre-deployment gate and ongoing governance framework for your AI editorial system.

sections.heading: Regulatory Compliance & Disclaimer Controls
sections.description: Ensure every piece of AI-generated financial content carries legally required disclosures and cannot be published without compliance review gates.

items.label: Mandatory Financial Disclaimer Injection
items.description: Configure the LLM pipeline to automatically append SEC-required disclaimers (e.g., 'This is not financial advice') to any content referencing investment products, returns, or market predictions. Validate disclaimer presence via post-generation regex checks before content is queued for publication.

items.label: Unlicensed Advice Detection Filter
items.description: Implement a secondary classifier that flags content containing specific buy/sell recommendations, yield guarantees, or personalized portfolio guidance. Route flagged content to a licensed compliance officer before any downstream use.

items.label: BSA/AML Sensitive Topic Blocklist
items.description: Maintain and version-control a blocklist of terms and content patterns associated with money laundering, structuring, or sanctions evasion. The LLM must refuse to generate content that could be construed as instructional for AML-prohibited activities.

items.label: SOX-Compliant Content Audit Trail
items.description: Log every content generation request, prompt, model version, output hash, reviewer identity, and approval timestamp in an immutable audit log. Retain logs for a minimum of 7 years to satisfy SOX Section 802 record-keeping requirements.

items.label: GDPR Data Minimization in Prompts
items.description: Prohibit injection of personally identifiable information (PII) such as customer names, account numbers, or transaction histories into LLM prompts. Enforce this via a PII scrubbing layer that runs before any prompt reaches the model API.

items.label: SEC Regulation FD Compliance Check
items.description: Before publishing AI-generated content referencing publicly traded entities, run an automated check to confirm no material non-public information (MNPI) sourced from internal systems has been included in the generation context.

items.label: Jurisdiction-Specific Regulatory Variant Handling
items.description: Tag all generated content with target jurisdiction metadata and apply region-specific compliance rules (e.g., FCA rules for UK audiences, MAS guidelines for Singapore). Maintain a jurisdiction rule matrix that is reviewed quarterly.

sections.heading: Content Accuracy & Factual Verification
sections.description: Financial misinformation carries direct liability. Establish automated and human verification workflows to ensure every factual claim in AI-generated content is accurate and sourced.

items.label: Real-Time Financial Data Source Grounding
items.description: Connect the LLM to verified, timestamped financial data feeds (e.g., Bloomberg, Reuters, official SEC EDGAR filings) via retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Every numerical claim—rates, fees, returns—must be traceable to a cited source in the output metadata.

items.label: Hallucination Detection Pipeline
items.description: Deploy a secondary verification model or rule-based checker that cross-references all factual assertions (statistics, regulatory citations, product features) against a curated knowledge base. Flag unverifiable claims with a confidence score below 0.85 for human review.

items.label: Regulatory Citation Accuracy Validation
items.description: When content references specific regulations (e.g., 'under PCI DSS 4.0 requirement 6.4'), run an automated lookup against the official regulatory text to confirm the citation is accurate and current. Reject content with stale or incorrect citations.

items.label: Product and Rate Freshness Check
items.description: Before publishing content that references specific product terms, APYs, credit card rates, or fee structures, validate against the live product catalog API. Implement a maximum content staleness threshold of 24 hours for rate-sensitive content.

items.label: Originality and Plagiarism Screening
items.description: Run all AI-generated content through a plagiarism detection tool (e.g., Copyscape or equivalent) before publication. Flag any content with more than 15% similarity to existing published material for rewrite or attribution.

items.label: Human Expert Review Gate for High-Stakes Content
items.description: Require sign-off from a licensed financial analyst or compliance officer for any content covering investment products, credit decisions, insurance, or regulatory guidance. Document reviewer credentials and approval in the audit log.

items.label: Contradiction Detection Across Content Library
items.description: Run a semantic consistency check between newly generated content and the existing published content library to detect contradictory claims about products, rates, or regulatory positions. Surface conflicts for editorial resolution before publication.

sections.heading: Brand Voice, Tone & Editorial Governance
sections.description: AI-generated content must consistently reflect the brand's approved voice, comply with editorial guidelines, and avoid language that could create legal or reputational exposure.

items.label: Brand Voice Style Guide Enforcement
items.description: Encode the brand's tone parameters (e.g., authoritative but accessible, no hyperbolic language, specific vocabulary preferences) as system-level prompt constraints. Run a post-generation tone classifier to score adherence and reject outputs below a defined threshold.

items.label: Prohibited Language and Claim Blocklist
items.description: Maintain a fintech-specific prohibited terms list covering guaranteed returns, risk-free, best-in-class without substantiation, and other FTC/SEC-scrutinized superlatives. Automatically redact or reject content containing these terms.

items.label: Readability and Accessibility Standards Check
items.description: Validate generated content against a target Flesch-Kincaid reading level appropriate for the intended audience (e.g., grade 8-10 for retail banking customers). Flag content that is overly complex or uses unexplained financial jargon.

items.label: SEO Compliance and Keyword Integrity Validation
items.description: Verify that SEO-targeted keywords are used accurately in context and do not create misleading meta descriptions or title tags. Ensure keyword density does not compromise regulatory clarity or introduce ambiguous financial claims.

items.label: Sensitive Topic Tone Calibration
items.description: For content covering debt, financial hardship, bankruptcy, or credit denial, enforce a compassionate and non-stigmatizing tone policy. Use a dedicated classifier trained on approved and rejected examples from your editorial team.

items.label: Multi-Stakeholder Editorial Approval Workflow
items.description: Implement a structured approval workflow requiring sign-off from Legal, Compliance, and Marketing before any AI-generated content is published. Track approval status, comments, and revision history in a content management system with role-based access.

items.label: AI Authorship Disclosure Policy
items.description: Define and enforce a clear policy on when AI-generated content must be disclosed as such to readers, in alignment with emerging FTC guidance and internal brand transparency standards. Apply disclosure labels consistently across all channels.

items.label: Version-Controlled Prompt and Template Registry
items.description: Store all production prompts, editorial templates, and system instructions in a version-controlled registry with change history. Require peer review and compliance sign-off before any prompt update is deployed to production.

sections.heading: Data Security, Privacy & Infrastructure Controls
sections.description: The content generation pipeline must handle financial data, customer context, and proprietary information with controls meeting PCI DSS, GDPR, and SOX infrastructure requirements.

items.label: PCI DSS Scope Exclusion for LLM Inputs
items.description: Architect the content generation pipeline so that cardholder data (PANs, CVVs, expiration dates) is never passed to the LLM or stored in prompt logs. Conduct a formal PCI DSS scoping exercise to confirm the AI system is out of cardholder data environment (CDE) scope.

items.label: Encryption of All Content Pipeline Data
items.description: Enforce TLS 1.3 for all data in transit between content generation services, APIs, and CMS platforms. Encrypt all stored prompt logs, generated drafts, and audit records using AES-256. Document encryption key management procedures in a FIPS 140-2 compliant key vault.

items.label: GDPR Data Subject Rights Integration
items.description: If any customer data is used to personalize content generation (e.g., segment-based content), implement mechanisms to honor data deletion and portability requests that propagate to training data, prompt caches, and generated content stores within 30 days.

items.label: LLM API Vendor Security Assessment
items.description: Conduct a formal third-party security assessment of any external LLM API provider (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI). Verify SOC 2 Type II certification, data processing agreements (DPAs) compliant with GDPR Article 28, and confirm the vendor does not train on your data.

items.label: Role-Based Access Control for Content Pipeline
items.description: Implement least-privilege RBAC across all components of the content generation system. Separate roles for prompt engineers, content reviewers, compliance approvers, and publishers. Audit access logs monthly and revoke stale permissions within 24 hours of role changes.

items.label: Content Generation Rate Limiting and Abuse Detection
items.description: Implement API rate limits and anomaly detection on the content generation pipeline to detect and block bulk generation attempts that could indicate prompt injection attacks, data exfiltration, or unauthorized use of the system.

items.label: Incident Response Plan for AI Content Failures
items.description: Define and test a specific incident response playbook for AI content failures including: mass publication of non-compliant content, hallucinated regulatory claims going live, or PII exposure via generated content. Include takedown procedures, regulator notification timelines, and customer communication templates.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: SEC Rule 17a-4 and SOX Section 802 require that all records related to financial communications—including AI-generated content drafts, prompts, approval workflows, and audit logs—be retained in a non-rewritable, non-erasable format for a minimum of 7 years. Ensure your content pipeline's logging infrastructure meets WORM (Write Once Read Many) storage standards.

Under GDPR Article 22, if AI-generated content is used to make or significantly influence automated decisions affecting individuals (e.g., personalized credit product recommendations), affected EU data subjects have the right to human review, explanation, and contestation. Document your legal basis for any such processing and build opt-out mechanisms into the content personalization layer.

PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 6.4 mandates that all public-facing web applications—including those serving AI-generated content—are protected against known web application attacks. Ensure your CMS and content delivery infrastructure undergoes annual penetration testing and that any AI-generated content forms or interactive elements are covered by your WAF policy.

BSA/AML regulations require financial institutions to maintain effective controls against facilitating financial crimes through any customer-facing channel, including digital content. AI-generated content that inadvertently describes, normalizes, or provides instructional detail on structuring transactions, smurfing, or sanctions evasion could expose the institution to FinCEN enforcement. Implement content-level AML risk scoring as part of your pre-publication compliance gate.

cta.heading: Automate Your Fintech Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI content generation pipeline against SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, and SEC requirements—catching compliance gaps before they become enforcement actions.
